status: first-pass evidence bundle exported from tracker rows

observations.observation_value: Focal cystic atrophy was largely restricted to the impact site, with substantial adjacent rostral/caudal cord mass preserved except in late syrinx cases.
observations.normalized_value: chronic SCI can preserve adjacent cord mass despite focal cystic loss

observations.observation_value: Human SCI caused large-diameter ventral-root axon loss at/near lesion levels and thin myelin in surviving large fibers.
observations.normalized_value: chronic SCI includes ventral-root axon loss and thin-myelin pathology
observations.notes: Tissue pathology not captured by MRI alone.

observations.observation_value: Five patients underwent MRI/electrophysiology-guided chronic scar resection and scaffold-cell implantation.
observations.normalized_value: chronic scar-resection anatomy can be mapped for human scaffold implantation

observations.observation_value: Pediatric SCI groups showed abnormal FA/RD in adjacent MRI-normal-appearing cord segments, with FA patterns related to AIS severity.
observations.normalized_value: DTI detects pediatric SCI abnormalities beyond conventional MRI

observations.observation_value: Lesion size contracted early, tissue bridges emerged, remote cord/CST degeneration progressed, and lesion/myelin metrics predicted long-term motor and SCIM recovery.
observations.normalized_value: acute-to-chronic MRI tracks lesion contraction, bridges, degeneration, and recovery prediction

observations.observation_value: Chronic SCI showed elevated lesion diffusion, lower FA at lesion, completeness-dependent FA, and altered upper-cervical diffusion.
observations.normalized_value: chronic SCI alters lesion and remote DTI metrics

observations.observation_value: Subject-specific injury-region DTI metrics were associated with residual motor and sensory impairment.
observations.normalized_value: subject-specific DTI metrics relate to motor/sensory impairment

observations.observation_value: Wider midsagittal tissue bridges at 1 month were associated with better lower-extremity motor and pinprick recovery at 1 year.
observations.normalized_value: thoracic tissue bridges predict lower-extremity motor and pinprick recovery

observations.observation_value: Cervical tissue bridge measures supported recovery-specific stratification and trial prognostication.
observations.normalized_value: cervical tissue bridges support recovery prognostication

observations.observation_value: Chronic SCI showed lower tNAA/mI and tCho/mI ratios at C2, associated with atrophy, tissue bridge severity, and worse sensory/motor outcomes.
observations.normalized_value: cervical MRS detects supralesional metabolic change linked to impairment

observations.observation_value: Longitudinal MRI detected degeneration above lesion in cranial CST and sensorimotor cortices with clinical outcome relevance.
observations.normalized_value: SCI causes remote CST and sensorimotor cortical degeneration

observations.observation_value: Early rostral cord and brain volume/microstructural changes predicted functional recovery.
observations.normalized_value: early qMRI rostral cord/brain metrics predict recovery

observations.observation_value: CST microstructural integrity related to spinal cord area and sensorimotor cortical reorganization.
observations.normalized_value: axonal integrity links spinal structure to cortical reorganization

observations.observation_value: Cord and cortical atrophy were associated with sensorimotor cortical reorganization and disability.
observations.normalized_value: cord/cortical atrophy associates with reorganization and disability

observations.observation_value: Paraplegic patients showed altered cortical representation of non-affected upper limb movements.
observations.normalized_value: chronic SCI alters motor cortical representation

observations.observation_value: Remote CST and dorsal column degeneration formed progressive spatial gradients after SCI.
observations.normalized_value: major spinal pathways degenerate progressively after SCI

observations.observation_value: Human SCI vascular pathology included hemorrhage, white-matter degeneration, venous occlusion, cavitation, and remote infarct-like lesions.
observations.normalized_value: human SCI includes vascular pathology and remote infarct-like lesions

observations.observation_value: Syrinx occurred in partially damaged segments and drainage did not yield satisfactory symptom relief despite radiographic drainage.
observations.normalized_value: post-traumatic syrinx can persist as chronic structural pathology

observations.observation_value: Progressive late post-traumatic syringomyelia was identified as a chronic structural complication after SCI.
observations.normalized_value: late post-traumatic syringomyelia is a chronic SCI complication

observations.observation_value: MRI anatomy is positioned as a clinical trial screening and outcome-measure tool.
observations.normalized_value: MRI supports SCI trial screening and endpoint selection

observations.observation_value: Independent spinal cord atrophy measures correlated with motor and sensory deficits.
observations.normalized_value: cord atrophy metrics correlate with impairment

observations.observation_value: Lateral corticospinal tract damage correlated with motor output in incomplete SCI.
observations.normalized_value: lateral CST damage correlates with motor output

observations.observation_value: Specific brain regions showed atrophy after cervical SCI.
observations.normalized_value: cervical SCI is associated with brain-region atrophy

observations.observation_value: Sensorimotor cortical plasticity changed during recovery following SCI.
observations.normalized_value: sensorimotor cortical plasticity changes during recovery

observations.observation_value: Human motor cortex plasticity was observed after cervical SCI.
observations.normalized_value: cervical SCI is associated with motor cortex plasticity

observations.observation_value: Motor cortex activation related to squeezing force after SCI.
observations.normalized_value: motor cortex activation relates to residual force

observations.observation_value: Sensory system atrophy was tracked and linked to outcome prediction.
observations.normalized_value: sensory pathway atrophy can support outcome prediction

observations.observation_value: Neurodegeneration above the level of SCI was tracked longitudinally.
observations.normalized_value: above-lesion neurodegeneration progresses after SCI

observations.observation_value: Neuroimaging can reveal pathological similarities and boundaries across traumatic and nontraumatic SCI.
observations.normalized_value: traumatic and nontraumatic SCI need separate imaging-pathology boundaries

observations.observation_value: qMRI metrics require cautious interpretation as indirect markers of myelin, iron, and tissue structure.
observations.normalized_value: qMRI markers are indirect and histology-informed

observations.observation_value: Chronic complete SCI showed supraspinal qMRI changes mainly as caudal brainstem volume reduction consistent with dorsal column Wallerian degeneration.
observations.normalized_value: complete SCI qMRI changes localize mainly to caudal brainstem volume reduction

observations.observation_value: Complete SCI showed altered metabolites in pons/cerebellum with correlations to clinical scores.
observations.normalized_value: supraspinal MRS detects metabolic changes linked to clinical scores

observations.observation_value: Imaging biomarkers are part of a broader diagnostic workup after traumatic SCI.
observations.normalized_value: imaging is one component of SCI biomarker workup

observations.observation_value: Neuroimaging tracks structural, microstructural, metabolic, and functional consequences of SCI.
observations.normalized_value: SCI neuroimaging spans structural, microstructural, metabolic, and functional domains

observations.observation_value: Tissue bridge ratio was associated with 6-minute walk distance in incomplete SCI.
observations.normalized_value: midsagittal tissue bridge ratio associates with walking ability

observations.observation_value: Trial-readiness literature frames imaging as screening and stratification rather than direct repair evidence.
observations.normalized_value: imaging trial readiness is screening/stratification evidence

author_claims.claim_text: Chronic human SCI lesion architecture can preserve adjacent cord tissue while focal cystic loss and syrinx/cyst pathology remain important compartments.
Ventral-root axon loss and thin-myelin pathology are human chronic SCI compartments relevant to motor output and remyelination boundaries.
Chronic scar resection and scaffold-cell implantation can be anatomically planned with MRI and electrophysiologic boundary mapping in humans.
Vascular pathology is part of human SCI lesion evolution and can include hemorrhage, venous occlusion, cavitation, and remote infarct-like lesions.
Post-traumatic syrinx and cystic complications should be preserved as distinct chronic structural complications.
DTI can detect abnormalities beyond conventional MRI-visible lesion morphology.
Subject-specific or tract-specific diffusion metrics relate to motor and sensory impairment after SCI.
Tissue bridge and lesion-geometry metrics are clinically meaningful prognostic MRI biomarkers.
Tissue bridge interpretation should preserve level, timing, etiology, completeness, and endpoint boundaries.
MRS can capture spinal or supraspinal metabolic changes related to impairment or clinical scores.
qMRI and longitudinal neuroimaging track remote degeneration and recovery predictors after SCI.
CST and dorsal column degeneration can occur above the lesion over time.
fMRI and cortical reorganization studies indicate remote plasticity after SCI.
Cortical reorganization claims should be linked to spinal lesion severity, tract integrity, or clinical impairment before consensus use.
Cord, sensory-system, and brain atrophy metrics are clinically relevant but indirect.
Imaging supports trial screening, endpoint selection, and stratification.
Pediatric, traumatic, ischemic, nontraumatic, complete, and incomplete SCI should not be collapsed without preserving cohort boundaries.
MRI evidence and autopsy pathology are complementary but not interchangeable.
DTI, qMRI, MRS, and fMRI are indirect biomarkers that require modality-specific confidence labels.
Surgical reconstruction and trial screening anatomy are feasibility or stratification evidence unless linked to repair outcomes.
Review and method rows should guide interpretation but should not replace primary cohort extraction.
Human pathology and imaging evidence define translation constraints for animal and biomaterial modules.
Preserved human tissue and surgical scar-resection anatomy may support reconstruction feasibility but do not prove regenerative permissiveness.
Ventral-root and myelin pathology links Module 6 to remyelination/white-matter repair, but remains human pathology evidence rather than intervention evidence.
Vascular and syrinx pathology link lesion architecture to chronic secondary structural damage.
Remote motor-system and sensory-system degeneration should be treated as active chronic pathology rather than only downstream disability.
Pediatric SCIWOMR highlights the limitation of conventional MRI for detecting clinically relevant cord pathology.
Lateral CST and tissue bridge metrics can support motor recovery stratification.
Metabolic imaging adds nonstructural clinical readouts to human SCI pathology assessment.
fMRI activation findings are lower-directness evidence for lesion anatomy unless tied to spinal metrics or outcomes.
Clinical trial imaging should be used as screening and stratification evidence before being treated as mechanistic repair evidence.
Module 6 consensus should stratify by modality, timing, injury level, etiology, completeness, and confidence.
Low-confidence abstract/source-page rows require full-text upgrade before strong numeric or mechanistic claims.
Chronic SCI imaging is not equivalent to histologic cell-state evidence.
Human evidence can set translational boundaries for implant, graft, and reconstruction planning.
Module 6B should preserve human evidence type and not mix animal imaging into human pathology consensus statements.

boundary_records.boundary_statement: Human lesion anatomy is multi-compartmental: focal cystic atrophy, preserved adjacent cord, tissue bridges, syrinx/cyst pathology, vascular pathology, ventral-root loss, and remote tract degeneration should not be collapsed into one lesion metric.
MRI, DTI, qMRI, MRS, fMRI, and autopsy pathology are complementary but not interchangeable; imaging-visible changes do not specify cell type or matrix composition without tissue evidence.
DTI, qMRI, MRS, and fMRI are indirect biomarkers and must retain modality-specific confidence labels, especially for abstract/source-page rows.
Brain and supraspinal changes should be linked back to spinal lesion severity, tract integrity, or clinical impairment before they are used as human lesion-architecture consensus evidence.
Surgical reconstruction and clinical trial screening anatomy are feasibility and stratification evidence unless linked to pathology, repair, or outcome evidence.
Pediatric SCIWOMR, traumatic adult SCI, ischemic SCI, nontraumatic SCI, complete SCI, and incomplete SCI should preserve age, etiology, level, and completeness boundaries.
Low-confidence abstract/source-page rows are acceptable for first-pass consensus readiness but require full-text upgrade before strong numeric, dose-like, or mechanism-specific SQL materialization.
Human pathology/imaging evidence sets translational constraints for reconstruction modules but does not by itself prove animal mechanism, implant compatibility, or regenerative permissiveness.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Human chronic SCI lesion architecture includes preserved adjacent tissue, focal cystic loss, scar-resection anatomy, and chronic syrinx/cyst complications; these compartments should be tracked separately.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Human chronic SCI lesion architecture includes preserved adjacent tissue, focal cystic loss, scar-resection anatomy, and chronic syrinx/cyst complications; these compartments should be tracked separately.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Human tissue pathology shows ventral-root axon/myelin injury and vascular/cystic compartments that are not reducible to MRI-visible lesion geometry.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Human tissue pathology shows ventral-root axon/myelin injury and vascular/cystic compartments that are not reducible to MRI-visible lesion geometry.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: DTI and tract-specific diffusion measures can reveal clinically relevant injury beyond conventional MRI and can relate to motor or sensory impairment.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: DTI and tract-specific diffusion measures can reveal clinically relevant injury beyond conventional MRI and can relate to motor or sensory impairment.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Tissue bridges and lesion-geometry measures are promising prognostic MRI biomarkers for recovery and walking ability, but interpretation must preserve level, etiology, timing, completeness, and endpoint boundaries.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Tissue bridges and lesion-geometry measures are promising prognostic MRI biomarkers for recovery and walking ability, but interpretation must preserve level, etiology, timing, completeness, and endpoint boundaries.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: qMRI, MRS, atrophy, and longitudinal neuroimaging support a model of chronic system-level pathology involving remote spinal, sensory, motor, brainstem, and metabolic changes.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: qMRI, MRS, atrophy, and longitudinal neuroimaging support a model of chronic system-level pathology involving remote spinal, sensory, motor, brainstem, and metabolic changes.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: fMRI and cortical reorganization studies support remote plasticity after SCI, but they are indirect lesion-bed evidence unless paired with spinal lesion, tract, or clinical impairment measures.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: fMRI and cortical reorganization studies support remote plasticity after SCI, but they are indirect lesion-bed evidence unless paired with spinal lesion, tract, or clinical impairment measures.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Human imaging can support surgical planning, trial screening, and stratification, but review and trial-readiness rows should not be treated as direct repair efficacy evidence.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Human imaging can support surgical planning, trial screening, and stratification, but review and trial-readiness rows should not be treated as direct repair efficacy evidence.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Module 6 consensus should remain human-specific, modality-stratified, confidence-labeled, and bounded by timing, injury level, etiology, completeness, and evidence directness.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Module 6 consensus should remain human-specific, modality-stratified, confidence-labeled, and bounded by timing, injury level, etiology, completeness, and evidence directness.
